remember: Students will be able to recall the geographical and social characteristics of the Indus Valley Civilization
Students will be able to identify the features of the Early Historic Period
Students will be able to describe the rise and fall of the Mahajanapadas
understand: Students will be able to explain the significance of the Indus Valley Civilization in the context of Indian history
Students will be able to analyze the social hierarchy of the Indus Valley Civilization
Students will be able to understand the impact of the Mauryan Empire on the development of Indian society
apply: Students will be able to apply the geographical and social characteristics of the Indus Valley Civilization to understand the development of Indian society
Students will be able to analyze the impact of the rise and fall of the Mahajanapadas on the political landscape of ancient India
Students will be able to evaluate the significance of the Early Historic Period in the context of Indian history
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the similarities and differences between the Indus Valley Civilization and the Vedic Period
Students will be able to evaluate the impact of the Mauryan Empire on the development of Indian society
Students will be able to compare the social and economic characteristics of the Indus Valley Civilization and the Early Historic Period

ncert_objectives: To understand the geography and climate of the Indus Valley Civilization and its impact on the development of society
To analyze the social hierarchy and economic system of the Indus Valley Civilization
To describe the rise and fall of the Mahajanapadas and their impact on the political landscape of ancient India
board_exam_objectives: To recall the geographical and social characteristics of the Indus Valley Civilization and the Early Historic Period
To explain the significance of the Indus Valley Civilization and the Mauryan Empire in the context of Indian history
To analyze the impact of the rise and fall of the Mahajanapadas on the political landscape of ancient India
